# Daily Report — 2026-05-25

## Task Completed
Added `list_sla_breaches` tool targeting the ServiceNow `task_sla` table. Each
record in `task_sla` tracks the real-time SLA state for an individual task
(incident, change request, problem, etc.).

### New tool: `list_sla_breaches`
- Queries `/api/now/table/task_sla` with `sysparm_display_value=true`
- Filters: `has_breached` (bool), `stage` (in_progress/breached/paused/completed),
  `table_name` (e.g. incident), `task_sys_id` (specific task), `sla_sys_id` (specific SLA definition)
- Returns normalised records: sys_id, task (display), sla (display), stage,
  has_breached, breach_time, start_time, end_time, business_duration, duration,
  percentage, table_name, created_on, updated_on
- Full pagination: `limit`, `offset`, `has_more`, `next_offset`, `count`
- Registered in `service_desk`, `system_administrator`, and `full` packages

### Also fixed: `list_slas` and `get_sla` registration
Both tools were implemented but missing from `tool_utils.py` (and therefore
unreachable by the MCP server). They are now properly registered with full
descriptions.

## Test Results
- Total tests: 1231 (19 new in test_sla_tools.py for `list_sla_breaches`)
- Coverage: 91% total; sla_tools.py 94%
- All passing: yes

## Security
System-level vulnerabilities in pip/setuptools/urllib3 (not project code); no
project dependency issues.

## Files Changed
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/sla_tools.py` — added `TASK_SLA_TABLE`, `TASK_SLA_FIELDS`, `_format_task_sla`, `ListSLABreachesParams`, `list_sla_breaches`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py` — export `list_sla_breaches`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — import + register `list_slas`, `get_sla`, `list_sla_breaches`
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — add `list_sla_breaches` (and `list_slas`/`get_sla`) to service_desk, system_administrator, full packages
- `tests/test_sla_tools.py` — add `TestFormatTaskSLA` (4 tests) and `TestListSLABreaches` (15 tests)
- `.github/daily-reports/2026-05-25.md` — this report

## Next Up
Add tests for `sla_tools.py` to push coverage above 94% (cover the `_format_task_sla` edge-cases and invalid-params path for `list_sla_breaches`), or implement a `get_sla_breach` tool for single-record lookup by sys_id.
